instructions: ✅ Select the correct option (A, B, C, or D).
✅ Each question has only ONE correct answer.
✅ This quiz covers C introduction, syntax, data types, operators, input/output, and basic rules.
✅ Try without seeing answers first, then verify using the answer field.

questions.question: C programming language is a ______ language.
questions.details: C is called a middle-level language because it supports both low-level and high-level programming features.

questions.question: Who developed the C programming language?
questions.details: C was developed by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct file extension for a C program?
questions.details: C source code files are saved with .c extension.

questions.question: Which function is the entry point of a C program?
questions.details: Execution of a C program starts from the main() function.

questions.question: Which header file is required for printf() and scanf() functions?
questions.details: printf() and scanf() are declared inside stdio.h (Standard Input Output).

questions.question: Which of the following is used to display output in C?
questions.details: printf() is used to print output on the screen in C.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to take input in C?
questions.details: scanf() is used to read input from the user.

questions.question: Which symbol is used to end a statement in C?
questions.details: Semicolon ';' is used to terminate a statement in C.

questions.question: Which of the following is a single-line comment in C?
questions.details: Single-line comments start with // in C (C99 and later).

questions.question: Which of the following is a multi-line comment in C?
questions.details: Multi-line comments are written between /* and */.

questions.question: Which data type is used to store whole numbers in C?
questions.details: int is used to store integer values.

questions.question: Which data type is used to store decimal numbers in C?
questions.details: float and double are used to store decimal values in C.

questions.question: Which data type is used to store a single character in C?
questions.details: char stores a single character like 'A'.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid variable name in C?
questions.details: Variable names can start with underscore, but not with digit or keyword.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to define a constant in C?
questions.details: const is used to make a variable constant (read-only).

questions.question: Which symbol is used for assignment in C?
questions.details: = assigns a value, while == compares values.

questions.question: Which operator is used for equality comparison in C?
questions.details: == checks whether two values are equal.

questions.question:
What will be the output of this code?
printf("%d", 5 + 3);
questions.details: 5 + 3 is integer addition, so output is 8.

questions.question: Which format specifier is used for int in printf()?
questions.details: %d is used for integer values.

questions.question: Which format specifier is used for float in printf()?
questions.details: %f is used to print float values.

questions.question: Which format specifier is used for char in printf()?
questions.details: %c prints a single character.

questions.question: Which format specifier is used for string in C?
questions.details: %s is used to print strings.

questions.question: Which operator is used to find remainder in C?
questions.details: Modulus operator % gives remainder.

questions.question:
What will be the output?
printf("%d", 10 / 3);
questions.details: 10 and 3 are integers so integer division gives 3.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to make decisions in C?
questions.details: if-else statements are used for conditional decision-making.

questions.question: Which loop is used when the number of iterations is known?
questions.details: for loop is generally used when iterations count is fixed.

questions.question: Which loop executes at least once even if condition is false?
questions.details: do-while checks condition after executing the body once.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to exit from a loop immediately?
questions.details: break terminates the loop immediately.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to skip the current iteration in a loop?
questions.details: continue skips the current iteration and goes to next.

questions.question: Which symbol is used to include a header file in C?
questions.details: Header files are included using #include.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid C function definition?
questions.details: C function syntax: returnType functionName(parameters) { ... }

questions.question: Which symbol is used to access the address of a variable?
questions.details: & gives the address of a variable (address-of operator).

questions.question: Which symbol is used to declare a pointer variable?
questions.details: * is used in pointer declaration: int *p;

questions.question: Which statement is TRUE about C language?

options.C: C is a procedural programming language

questions.details: C is procedural and structured programming language.

questions.question: Which of these is used to store multiple values of same type?
questions.details: Arrays store multiple values of the same datatype.

questions.question: Which is the correct way to declare an integer array of size 5?
questions.details: Array declaration syntax: datatype name[size];

questions.question: Which function is used to find length of a string?
questions.details: strlen() is defined in string.h and returns length of string.

questions.question: Which header file is needed for strlen() function?
questions.details: String handling functions like strlen() are in string.h.

questions.question:
What is the output?
printf("%c", 'A');
questions.details: %c prints the character itself, so output is A.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a C keyword?
questions.details: printf is a function, not a keyword.
